findings.title: Technology Fingerprinting
findings.description: Passive identification of technologies used by the target
findings.remediation: Use this information to correlate against known CVEs and version-specific vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Open Network Port Detected: 80 (HTTP)
findings.description: The service HTTP is exposed on TCP port 80.

evidence.banner:
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Date: Wed, 10 Dec 2025 06:46:01 GMT
Content-Length: 68
Connection: close
Cache-Control: private, max-age=0, no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, post-check=0, pre-check=0
Referrer-Policy: same-origin
Expires: Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT
Proxy-Status: Cloudflare-Proxy;error=http_request_error
CF-RAY: 9abac86609d4fa17-ORD

findings.remediation: If HTTP is not required to be publicly accessible, restrict access via firewall rules. Otherwise, enforce authentication, encryption, and continuous monitoring.
findings.attack_scenario: An attacker could directly interact with the exposed HTTP service on port 80. If authentication is weak or misconfigured, this could allow unauthorized system access, data extraction, or full infrastructure compromise.
findings.defense_strategy: Exposed services should be restricted using network firewalls, access control lists (ACLs), and zero-trust segmentation. Only systems that explicitly require access should be permitted.
findings.mitigation_plan: The IT team should review firewall rules, confirm that the exposed service is required, enforce strong authentication, disable unused services, and verify remediation via rescanning.

findings.title: TLS Configuration on Port 443
findings.description: TLS is enabled on port 443 using protocol TLSv1.3 with cipher TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 (256-bit).
findings.remediation: Renew certificates before expiration and ensure only modern TLS protocols and strong ciphers are enabled.
findings.attack_scenario: If TLS is misconfigured (expired certificate, weak protocols, or poor ciphers), attackers may intercept or manipulate traffic, downgrade encryption, or perform man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.defense_strategy: Use strong TLS configurations with modern protocols (TLS 1.2+), valid certificates, and trusted certificate authorities. Enforce HSTS where applicable.
findings.mitigation_plan: The IT team should ensure all public services use modern TLS versions, renew certificates before expiration, disable weak protocols, and verify configurations through continuous monitoring.
